name: Col de Larrieu Loop
description: Cycle the difficult 21.6-mile Col de Larrieu route in the Pyrenees, featuring 2161 feet of climbing and scenic views of the Arbas valley.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Fougaron, located in the Haute-Garonne department, offers access to the Pyrenees mountains and their foothills. The region features diverse valleys, lush greenways, and quiet roads suitable for cycling. This area is characterized by a verdant environment, providing varied terrain for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Fougaron The most popular touring cycling route is Col de Larrieu Loop, a 21.6 miles (34.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 24 minutes to complete, featuring significant elevation gain in the Pyrenean foothills. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Héran and the Col des Pères – Bear Fountain. loop from Fougaron, a difficult 13.6 miles (22.0 km) path that traverses mountainous terrain and valleys. Local touring cyclists also love the Bike loop from Arbas, a 10.2 miles (16.4 km) trail leading through diverse valleys and quiet roads, often completed in about 1 hour 13 minutes. Touring cycling around Fougaron is defined by Pyrenees mountains, diverse valleys, and greenways. The network offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels. The routes in Fougaron are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 700 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Fougaron's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Fougaron?
A: There are over 60 touring cycling routes around Fougaron, offering a wide range of options for exploring the Pyrenees mountains and their foothills. The komoot community has contributed to mapping these diverse trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Fougaron?
A: The terrain around Fougaron is characterized by the majestic Pyrenees mountains, diverse valleys, lush greenways, and picturesque, quiet roads. You'll encounter iridescent green and vibrant mountains, grass, and trees, along with wild rivers and dramatic gorges, providing a rich and verdant environment for touring.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes for beginners or families in Fougaron?
A: While many routes in Fougaron are moderate to difficult, the region's greenways and sections of routes like the Vélosud are generally more accessible. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35681661" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike loop from Arbas&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 10.2-mile (16.4 km) trail that leads through diverse valleys and quiet roads, suitable for those looking for a less strenuous ride.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders near Fougaron?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Fougaron offers routes with significant elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e930253385/col-de-larrieu-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de Larrieu Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 21.6-mile (34.8 km) trail with substantial climbing. Additionally, the region is close to iconic Pyrenean passes like Col de Peyresourde, Col du Portillon, and Col de Menté, which have featured in the Tour de France and offer intense climbs and spectacular views.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling options from Fougaron?
A: Yes, Fougaron provides access to significant long-distance cycling routes. The Vélosud is a 650km route traversing the gentle foothills of the Pyrenees, with sections in Haute-Garonne. Another excellent option is the Transgarona Cycle Route, which links France and Spain, allowing for extended exploration of the Haut-Garonne Pyrenees.

Q: What natural attractions or viewpoints can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Along the routes, you can discover several natural attractions and viewpoints. Notable highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5878841" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mail de Laroque&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2735047" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Terreblanque Cave&lt;/a&gt;, and various segments on plateaus offering panoramic views, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5878850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Segment on the plateau&lt;/a&gt;. You'll also encounter several mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/128889" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portet-d'Aspet pass&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1530581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de Larrieu (704 m)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Fougaron?
A: The best season for touring cycling in Fougaron is generally from spring to autumn. During these months, the weather is more favorable, with milder temperatures and less chance of snow in the higher elevations, making for more comfortable and enjoyable rides through the verdant landscapes.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available around Fougaron?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Fougaron are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e930253385/col-de-larrieu-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de Larrieu Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24670906" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Héran and the Col des Pères – Bear Fountain. loop from Fougaron&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Fougaron?
A: The touring cycling routes in Fougaron are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 700 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the stunning Pyrenean scenery, diverse valleys, and quiet roads.

Q: Are there any specific services for cyclists in the Fougaron area?
A: Fougaron and the surrounding region are recognized for their cycling tourism. You can find guide services like "MAX COLOMBEL: MTB GUIDE PYRÉNÉES" to assist with exploring the terrain. The broader Haute-Garonne area also offers guided bike trips, as highlighted by &lt;a href="https://www.hautegaronnetourism.com/moving-going-out/to-live-fully/guided-bike-trips/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Haute-Garonne Tourism&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through mountain passes in Fougaron?
A: Absolutely. The region around Fougaron is home to several notable mountain passes that are popular with touring cyclists. You can ride through passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/128889" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portet-d'Aspet pass&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1530581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de Larrieu (704 m)&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3118859" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col des Pères&lt;/a&gt;, offering both challenging climbs and rewarding views.

Q: Are there any routes that are particularly scenic or offer great views?
A: Many routes in Fougaron offer spectacular panoramic views, especially those that ascend into the Pyrenean foothills or cross mountain passes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15017565" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de Larrieu (704 m) – Viewpoint loop from Arbas&lt;/a&gt; is a longer, difficult route specifically designed to include a viewpoint, rewarding cyclists with expansive vistas of the surrounding mountains and valleys.
tips: Nice climb on a road in good condition. The highest point is 707m above sea level. A must do if you are in the area

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the climb to Col de Larrieu?
faqs.answer: The climb is generally considered difficult for touring cyclists. There are two main approaches: from Aspet, it's a Category 3 climb with an average gradient of 4.9% over 5.5 km, reaching a maximum of 9.9%. From Barat, it's steeper, a Category 2 or 3 climb, averaging 6.6% over 4.6 km with sections up to 11.9%. The Pyrenees are known for inconsistent gradients, so be prepared for steeper sections.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the ascent to Col de Larrieu?
faqs.answer: The route involves significant climbing on paved roads, characteristic of Pyrenean mountain passes. The gradients can be inconsistent, with some sections reaching 12-14%. For the steeper Barat ascent, a compact mountain gearing system (e.g., a 34-tooth chainring with a 30, 32, or 34-tooth cassette) is recommended to manage the demanding sections.

faqs.question: Are there different approaches to the Col de Larrieu, and how do they compare?
faqs.answer: Yes, there are two primary ascents. The climb from Aspet is 5.5 km long with an average gradient of 4.9% and a maximum of 9.9%. The ascent from Barat is shorter at 4.6 km but steeper, with an average gradient of 6.6% and a maximum of 11.9%. The Barat side is generally considered more challenging and may require specific gearing.

faqs.question: What scenic views can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: Cyclists can enjoy picturesque views of the Arbas valley and charming villages nestled in the Piedmont Pyrenees. The majestic Pic de Paloumère often provides a scenic backdrop to the ride, showcasing the breathtaking vistas of the broader Pyrenean landscape.

faqs.question: Are there any notable features or other activities near the Col de Larrieu?
faqs.answer: The Col de Larrieu (704 m) is a recognized climb frequently included in local cycling routes, such as the 'Col de Larrieu and Piémont' circuit. The surrounding area is also popular for other outdoor activities like paragliding, caving, mountain biking, and hiking. A dedicated mountain biking 'flow trail' called La Palombière originates nearby.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Pyrenees region generally offers good cycling conditions from late spring through early autumn. Specific weather conditions can vary, so it's always best to check the forecast closer to your planned visit. Avoid winter months due to potential snow and ice.

faqs.question: Is the Col de Larrieu route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating and significant elevation gain with steep gradients, this route is generally not recommended for beginners or young families. It requires a good level of fitness and appropriate cycling gear, especially for the steeper ascents.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: This route is primarily a road cycling climb. While there aren't specific restrictions against dogs on public roads, it's not a typical 'dog-friendly trail' in the hiking sense. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure they are safely secured and consider the traffic and road conditions.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Col de Larrieu. It is a public road accessible to cyclists.

faqs.question: Where can I park when cycling the Col de Larrieu?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas directly at the Col de Larrieu summit are limited, you can typically find parking in the nearby villages of Aspet or Barat, which serve as common starting points for the climb. From there, you can cycle to the pass.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or accommodation options nearby?
faqs.answer: Yes, the villages surrounding the Col de Larrieu, such as Aspet and Barat, offer various amenities. You can find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options in these charming Pyrenean foothills villages, perfect for pre- or post-ride refreshments and stays.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Pedals d'Occitània, Chemin de Las Pélados, Chemin Col de Louzet, Place du Pré Commun, and Chemin des Sources. This area is a hub for various cycling and outdoor activities.
